mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1.0% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal, with lower rates directly supporting equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% indicates extreme monetary accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 7.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions cascade through equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE > 7.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand that portends lower corporate revenues and earnings. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates when sales growth turns negative, predicting negative equity returns over 20 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 415695 million requires trend analysis for activation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances where RSAFS growth is negative, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) index below 98.0 combined with rising unemployment indicates supply-side weakness and labor market slack. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative returns because simultaneous supply contraction and labor deterioration signal stagflationary pressures that compress equity multiples over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where both INDPRO < 98.0 AND UNRATE > 7.5%, or the joint interaction term has Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 900 thousand units annualized signals residential construction weakness that reduces aggregate demand, employment multipliers, and forward GDP growth. This mechanism predicts negative 20-day equity returns as forward growth expectations decline in housing-constrained regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 861.0 thousand units below threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of periods where HOUST < 900, or the correlation between HOUST levels and subsequent 20-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 50 thousand persons per month combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals stagflationary labor market dynamics. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative returns because wage-price spiral pressures force monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134021 thousand requires growth rate calculation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction term has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth trajectory combined with retail sales momentum (RSAFS) determines aggregate demand strength. In late-cycle expansion regimes where GDPC1 growth slows below 2% annualized while RSAFS remains elevated, equity valuations compress as terminal growth expectations decline, producing negative 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2% while RSAFS remains above 410000 million
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13652.5 billion requires growth rate calculation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 2% AND RSAFS > 410000, or the interaction Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) index above 231 combined with federal funds rate below 1.0% indicates monetary policy lag where real rates remain deeply negative. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive equity returns over 5 trading days in inflation-accommodation regimes because negative real rates compress bond yields and support equity risk premiums, but fails in tightening regimes.
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with elevated inflation regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the mechanism reverses sign when FEDFUNDS rises above 2.0%.
